field.label: Production Environment
field.tooltip: Yes for live sites, No for dev/staging on non-standard domains.
field.comment:
Yes = this is your live site. The module checks the licence key against the current domain. If invalid, the module silently disables itself.
No = this is dev/staging. The module runs at full features without a licence — use this on non-standard dev domains the auto-detector misses.
Default: Yes.

field.label: License Key
field.tooltip: Paste your purchase-email key here. Bound to the production domain.
field.comment:
Paste the key from your purchase email. Tied to your live website's address.
Test or staging sites don't need a key — the module detects them automatically.
www. doesn't matter — one key works whether your address is shop.com or www.shop.com.

field.label: Enable Module
field.tooltip: Master on/off switch. No = module silently does nothing.
field.comment:
Master on/off switch.
Yes = the module runs: fast shipping options are hidden at checkout for ineligible carts, and the badge shows on product pages.
No = the module does nothing. Useful if you want to quickly check whether a checkout issue is caused by this module.

field.label: Express / Next-Day Shipping Methods
field.tooltip: Tick the fast shipping options you want to HIDE when the cart has any item that can't ship next day.
field.comment:
Tick the fast shipping options (next-day, express, overnight) that should be hidden at checkout when the cart contains any item that can't ship next day.

How to pick: click an option to add or remove it. Use the red Clear all link below the field to reset every choice.

Can't see one of your shipping options? Some shipping add-ons register options in their own way and won't appear here automatically. Paste their code into the Custom Express Method Codes field below — it'll appear in this dropdown next time.

What happens at checkout: when any cart item is not next-day eligible, the options you tick here disappear from the shipping list. Slower options stay available so the customer can still complete the order.

⚠️ Don't tick everything. If you tick every option, ineligible carts will have no shipping choices and customers can't check out. The module restores the full list if that happens, but the right pattern is to tick only your paid express / next-day options.

field.label: Custom Express Method Codes
field.tooltip: For shipping options that aren't appearing in the dropdown above. Paste their codes here, separated by commas.
field.comment:
Use this field when one of your shipping options doesn't appear in the dropdown above. Common with custom shipping add-ons — the option works at checkout but doesn't show up in this list by default.

How to use: paste the shipping option code(s), separated by commas. Example: custom_carrier_nextday, mycarrier_express
Codes you paste here automatically appear in all three dropdowns on this page (Express, Standard, Backorder Express) under a "Custom / Additional Codes" group, so you can pick them like any built-in option.

Don't know the code? Ask your developer or the shipping add-on's support — they'll have a list of the codes the add-on uses.

field.label: Standard / Slow Shipping Methods (allow list)
field.tooltip: Optional. Tick the slower options that should still be offered when a cart can't ship next day. Leave empty to use the Express list above instead.
field.comment:
Optional — easier alternative to the Express list above. Pick the slower / standard shipping options you want customers to see when their cart can't ship next day.

How it behaves:
  • If you tick options here: when a cart has any item that can't ship next day, customers see only the options you ticked. Everything else is hidden. (The Express list above is ignored in this mode.)
  • If you leave this empty (default): NDE falls back to the Express list above — it hides the options you ticked there and keeps everything else visible.
Why use this instead of the Express list? It's a clearer way to configure NDE: "these are the slow options I want customers to see" is easier to think about than "these are the fast options I want to hide". And when you add a new shipping option to your store later, it won't accidentally appear on ineligible carts — only the options you've explicitly ticked here will show.

Pick whichever style you prefer. Custom shipping options pasted into the Custom Express Method Codes or Custom Standard Method Codes field appear in this dropdown too.

field.label: Custom Standard Method Codes
field.tooltip: For slow / standard shipping options that aren't appearing in the dropdown above. Paste their codes here, comma-separated.
field.comment:
Same idea as Custom Express Method Codes, but for slower / standard shipping options. Paste the codes of any standard shipping options that don't appear in the dropdown above, separated by commas.
Codes you paste here count as part of the Standard allow list AND show up in every other dropdown on this page under "Custom / Additional Codes".

field.label: Click & Collect / In-Store Pickup Methods
field.tooltip: Tick any in-store pickup options. If you do, customers won't see them when their cart has anything that's out of local stock.
field.comment:
Optional — for stores with physical shops. Tick the shipping options you use for in-store pickup (Click & Collect, Curbside Pickup, etc.).

What happens if you tick a method here: when a customer's cart has any item that is out of local stock, that pickup method is hidden at checkout — because you can't pick up something that isn't on your shelf. The next-day rules above run separately and aren't affected.

Why it's separate from the next-day rules: a product can be next-day-eligible via a drop-ship supplier, but it can never be Click & Collect without local stock. This setting respects that.

Leave empty if you don't offer in-store pickup. Nothing changes, no methods get hidden.

field.label: Custom Click & Collect Method Codes
field.tooltip: For pickup options that aren't appearing in the dropdown above. Paste their codes here, comma-separated.
field.comment: Same idea as Custom Express Method Codes, but for in-store pickup options. Paste codes of any pickup methods that don't appear in the dropdown above, separated by commas. Codes pasted here count as part of the Click & Collect list AND show up in every other dropdown on this page under "Custom / Additional Codes".

field.label: Show Badge on Product Page
field.tooltip: Both = show green badge for eligible + grey for ineligible. Eligible only = show green only. Never = hide both badges from product pages.
field.comment:
Controls the green/grey badge on the product page.
Both (default) = show "Next Day Eligible" (green) for eligible products AND "Standard Delivery" (grey) for ineligible ones. Maximum transparency.
Eligible only = show the green badge only. Hides the grey badge so you don't draw negative attention to ineligible products.
Never = no badge on the product page. The shipping rule at checkout still works — this just hides the visual indicator.

The grey badge is also hidden on products that can't be added to cart (out of stock with no backorders), so it never appears alongside an "Out of Stock" label.

field.label: Badge Label — Eligible
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the green eligible badge.
field.comment: Text shown on the green badge for products that qualify for next-day delivery. This text can be changed per store / per language.

field.label: Badge Label — Ineligible
field.tooltip: Customer-facing label for the grey ineligible badge. Default: "Standard Delivery".
field.comment: Text shown on the grey badge for products that do NOT qualify for next-day delivery. Only used when Show Badge on Product Page = Both. Can be different per store / language.

field.label: Auto-Enable Backorders for Drop-Ship Products
field.tooltip: Yes = ticking Drop-Ship Eligible also sets Magento Backorders to "Allow Qty Below 0".
field.comment:
Closes a UX gap when you flag products as Drop-Ship Eligible.

Yes (default) = when a product's Drop-Ship Eligible attribute is set to Yes and saved, the module automatically sets that product's Advanced Inventory → Backorders to "Allow Qty Below 0" too. This keeps the storefront usable: products that show as "Next Day Eligible" via drop-ship will also have an Add-to-Cart button when local stock is zero.
Flipping Drop-Ship Eligible back to No reverts the backorder setting to "Use Config".

No = the module never touches your products' backorder settings. You manage them manually via Advanced Inventory. Note: drop-ship products with local stock of zero will then show as Out of Stock and lose their Add-to-Cart button.

field.label: How Drop-Ship Works
field.comment:
When a product is out of local stock, it's normally marked ineligible and next-day shipping is blocked. The exception: if a product has Drop-Ship Eligible = Yes set on its edit page (under the eTechFlow Shipping attribute group), it remains next-day eligible even when local stock is zero. This is for products fulfilled directly by a supplier who ships on your behalf the same day.

Bulk-edit tip: to flag many products at once, go to Catalog → Products, filter as needed, tick the products, and use Actions → Update attributes → Drop-Ship Eligible = Yes.

field.label: Drop-Ship Source
field.tooltip: How the module decides if a product is drop-ship. Pick "Manual tickbox" if your store is simple. Pick "Supplier-based" if products have multiple suppliers.
field.comment:
How does the module know a product ships from a supplier (counts as next-day) rather than shipping from your own stock?

Manual tickbox (default, recommended) — you tick the Drop-Ship Eligible checkbox on each product yourself. Simple and explicit. Best for most stores.

Supplier-based — the module reads supplier info already on the product (e.g. attributes like "Supplier 1", "Supplier 2"). Use this if you already track multiple suppliers per product and only some of them ship next-day.

The manual tickbox always wins. Setting Drop-Ship Eligible = Yes on a product forces it to count as next-day no matter which mode you pick.

field.label: Supplier Attribute Pairs
field.tooltip: Tell the module which product attributes hold supplier info. One line per supplier slot. Format: tickbox-attribute:name-attribute
field.comment:
For each supplier slot on your products, tell the module two things: which attribute holds the on/off tickbox, and which holds the supplier name. One slot per line, separated by a colon: tickbox-attribute:name-attribute

Example — if your store has three supplier slots called Supplier 1, Supplier 2, and Supplier 3, with tickbox attributes s1_active, s2_active, s3_active and name attributes s1, s2, s3: 
s1_active:s1
s2_active:s2
s3_active:s3
Order matters — the top line is your highest-priority supplier (the one you'd ship from first).

Lines starting with # are ignored, so you can leave comments. Not sure which attribute codes you have? In the admin, go to Stores → Attributes → Product and look at the "Attribute Code" column.

field.label: Qualifying Supplier Names
field.tooltip: List the supplier names that can ship next-day. One per line. Capitalisation doesn't matter.
field.comment:
Of all the suppliers you work with, which ones can actually ship next-day? List them here, one name per line. The module will mark a product as next-day eligible only if it's sourced from one of these suppliers.

Example — if "Auto Remote" and "FastFreight" can ship next-day, but "Remkeys" can't: 
Auto Remote
FastFreight Inc.
Match is case-insensitive and ignores extra spaces, so "auto remote", "Auto Remote", and " AUTO REMOTE " all match.

Leave empty and supplier mode does nothing — products fall back to the manual Drop-Ship Eligible tickbox.

field.label: Supplier Match Mode
field.tooltip: Choose how to read multiple suppliers on a product. "First active wins" matches real fulfillment. "Any active qualifying" is more generous for marketing.
field.comment:
When a product has multiple suppliers ticked, which one decides eligibility?

First active wins (recommended) — use the top-ranked supplier that's ticked on the product. If THAT supplier can ship next-day → eligible. If not → not eligible.
Pick this if your fulfillment actually cascades top-to-bottom (you always ship from Supplier 1 if you can, otherwise Supplier 2, etc.). It's the most honest answer for customers.

Any active qualifying — eligible if ANY ticked supplier can ship next-day, regardless of which one you'd actually ship from.
Pick this if you want the product page badge to be more optimistic for marketing, and you handle the actual supplier choice separately.

Stores upgrading from before v1.6.3 stay on "Any active qualifying" automatically so nothing changes silently. Switch to "First active wins" when you're ready.

field.label: Also hide express options when cart has backorder items
field.tooltip: Optional second rule. Yes = also hide fast shipping when the cart contains items the customer is backordering.
field.comment:
Optional second rule, on top of the next-day rule above.

Yes = whenever the cart contains a backorder item (out of stock with backorders allowed, or ordered quantity larger than the available stock), the shipping options you tick below are hidden at checkout.
No (default) = this rule is off. The next-day rule above still runs independently.

field.label: Express Options to Hide for Backorder Carts
field.tooltip: Tick the express options to hide when the cart contains backorder items.
field.comment:
Tick the express / fast shipping options to hide at checkout whenever the cart contains backorder items.

Kept separate from the Express list at the top of this page so you can target different options for the two rules — e.g. you might hide only DHL Express for backorder carts, but hide DHL Express AND Royal Mail Special Delivery for any ineligible cart.

⚠️ Same warning as the Express list above — don't tick every option. Always leave at least one slower / free option visible so backorder customers can still complete checkout.

field.label: Custom Express Codes for Backorder Rule
field.tooltip: Same idea as Custom Express Method Codes — for shipping options not in the dropdown above.
field.comment: Same idea as Custom Express Method Codes in the General Settings group — paste shipping option codes (comma-separated) that aren't appearing in the dropdown above. Codes you paste here are merged with whatever you ticked above.

field.label: Skip drop-ship products from this rule
field.tooltip: Yes (default) = drop-ship products ignore the backorder rule because the supplier ships them direct.
field.comment:
Yes (default) = products marked Drop-Ship Eligible = Yes skip this backorder rule. They aren't really on backorder from the customer's point of view because the supplier ships them directly.
No = the backorder rule applies even to drop-ship products. Only use this if your suppliers have unreliable shipping speeds.

field.label: Show Notice at Checkout
field.tooltip: Yes = dismissible banner explains why express options are gone.
field.comment:
Yes (default) = a dismissible banner appears on the shipping step of checkout whenever either shipping rule fires. Tells the customer why the express options aren't available. Reduces "where's my faster shipping?" support tickets.
No = no banner. Methods are still removed silently.

field.label: Notice Style
field.tooltip: Banner colour: warning (amber) / info (blue) / error (red).
field.comment: Colour scheme for the banner: Warning (amber, default), Info (blue), or Error (red). Pick the tone that matches your brand and the urgency you want to convey.

field.label: Notice Title
field.tooltip: Bold heading on the banner.
field.comment: Bold heading at the top of the banner. Default: "Next day delivery unavailable". Per-store-view configurable for multi-language stores.

field.label: Notice Message
field.tooltip: Body text under the title. Keep generic — applies to both rules.
field.comment:
Body text under the title. Default: "One or more items in your cart is not eligible for next day delivery. Standard shipping options are available."
Same message shows for both rules (next-day OR backorder). If you want rule-specific wording, keep it generic — e.g. "Express shipping unavailable for one or more items in your cart."
